title: catesbaea spinosa
intro: Catesbaea spinosa, also known as the thorny elephant ear, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to the Caribbean and Central America, and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Catesbaea spinosa can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been used in herbal remedies to promote wound healing and reduce pain.
